rulings.Case Title: TONY FU VS. DEMAS YAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Mandatory Dismissal Of Plaintiff'S Action
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 12, 2022 line 1. DEFENDANT KAMAN LIU's Motion For Mandatory Dismissal Of Plaintiff'S Action OFF CALENDAR, insufficient notice per CCP 1005 and CCP 1013. The Court notes that while Notice of Bankruptcy Stay is a part of the record in this case, neither the Notice nor the stay are addressed in the moving papers. Instead, they are addressed for the first time in the reply without any reference or analysis of the Bankruptcy Code 362(c) cited by the Bankruptcy Court as the law governing plaintiff's bankruptcy case in August 30, 2017 order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINE DOVER VS. EILEEN DOHERTY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 12, 2022 line 5. DEFENDANTs EILEEN DOHERTY and MATTHEW NICHOLS' DEMURRER to COMPLAINT off calendar. This demurrer was submitted for filing on April 29, 2022. Papers submitted included a 3-page Notice of Demurrer; a 3-page demurrer and a 42-page declaration. No points and authorities submitted for filing. On June 13, 2022 moving party filed points and authorities. On July 29, 2022 the Court provided the moving party an opportunity to explain the late-filing. Moving party appears to suggest that June 13, 2022 filing was a "courtesy copy." A courtesy copy is a true and correct hard copy of a document previously/simultaneously submitted for filing provided to the department per SFRL 2.7B. No explanation provided for the late filing. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: HIGH POINT FINANCE LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. VIOLIE D. PASTOR, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE OF ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 12, 2022 line 7. DEFENDANT VIOLIE PASTOR's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is sustained to breach of contract cause of action for Plaintiff to explain the inconsistency of allegations in the complaint and the exhibit to complaint (Complaint alleges that that parties orally agreed that Plaintiff would have 100% ownership interest in the Subject Property versus Grant Deed reflects that parties "continue to hold the same proportionate interest" in the Property). These allegations are material and go to the heart of the dispute - the terms of the contract. Demurrer is otherwise overruled to this cause of action, including statute of frauds, which was satisfied by allegations of debt payment and replacement property selection. Demurrer to other causes of action was opposed and not further pursued in the written reply, unlike the demurrer to the breach of contract cause of action. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
